title: EaROS Solution Architecture Artifact
description: Schema for EaROS-governed Solution Architecture documents. Derived from the required_evidence fields of the core meta-rubric (EAROS-CORE-002) and the solution-architecture profile (EAROS-SOL-001). Each section maps to a profile criterion: solution_options → SOL-01 (optioning and rationale, major gate), quality_attributes → SOL-02 (quality attribute treatment, critical gate), operational_model + raid → SOL-03 (delivery and operability readiness). A well-completed document produces the evidence an assessor needs for every SOL criterion and the full core rubric.

kind.title: Document kind
kind.description: Fixed discriminator. Always 'artifact' for architecture documents.

artifact_type.title: Artifact type
artifact_type.description: Locked to 'solution_architecture'. Binds this document to profile EAROS-SOL-001 alongside the core meta-rubric.

metadata.title: Document metadata
metadata.description: Administrative and identification fields. Maps to evidence required by core criteria STK-01 (purpose, stakeholders, decision_context) and MNT-01 (owner, version, last_updated, change_log). All required fields must be present for a score above 1 on MNT-01.

title.title: Title
title.description: Full title of the solution architecture. Should name the business capability, system, or programme. Example: 'Customer Onboarding Platform — Target Solution Architecture'.

status.title: Status
status.description: Lifecycle status: 'draft' (in progress), 'candidate' (ready for Architecture Board review), 'approved' (formally accepted), 'deprecated' (superseded).

author.title: Author
author.description: Individual who authored or last significantly updated this document.

owner.title: Owner
owner.description: Named individual or team with ongoing stewardship responsibility. Evidence for core criterion MNT-01. Example: 'Solution Architecture, Customer Domain'.

effective_date.title: Effective date
effective_date.description: Date from which this version is the active reference. Format: YYYY-MM-DD.

next_review_date.title: Next review date
next_review_date.description: Date by which this document must be reviewed and either reconfirmed or updated. Evidence for MNT-01 score ≥ 3. Format: YYYY-MM-DD.

last_updated.title: Last updated
last_updated.description: Date of the most recent substantive change. Format: YYYY-MM-DD.

purpose.title: Purpose statement
purpose.description: One or two sentences stating what this solution is for and what decision it supports. Evidence for core criterion STK-01 (major gate). Example: 'Replace the legacy onboarding workflow with a cloud-native solution supporting same-day account opening for retail customers.'

decision_context.title: Decision or usage context
decision_context.description: The governance or project context in which this solution is proposed — the Architecture Board review it supports, the programme it belongs to, the delivery milestone it targets. Evidence for STK-01.

stakeholders.title: Stakeholders
stakeholders.description: Named individuals or roles with a significant concern in this solution. Evidence for core criterion STK-01 (named stakeholder list required for score ≥ 2).

concerns.title: Concerns addressed
concerns.description: What this stakeholder needs from this document. Evidence for STK-02.

change_log.title: Change log
change_log.description: History of substantive changes to this document, most recent first. Evidence for MNT-01 — at least one entry is required for score ≥ 3.

sections.title: Document sections
sections.description: Substantive content of the solution architecture. Every required section maps to one or more EaROS criteria; omitting a required section will lower the corresponding score.

reading_guide.title: Reading guide
reading_guide.description: Maps document sections to stakeholder concerns. Evidence for core criterion STK-02. A complete mapping is required for STK-02 score ≥ 3.

scope.title: Scope and boundary
scope.description: Defines exactly what is and is not covered by this solution. Without clear scope, the artifact may be rated 'not_reviewable'. Evidence for core criterion SCP-01 (critical gate — score < 2 triggers not_reviewable status).

statement.title: Scope statement
statement.description: Concise, unambiguous statement of what this solution architecture covers.

out_of_scope.title: Out of scope
out_of_scope.description: Items intentionally excluded, with rationale or reference. Required for SCP-01 score > 2.

boundary_definition.title: Boundary definition
boundary_definition.description: Description of the system context boundary — what is inside the solution vs. what is external.

assumptions.title: Contextual assumptions
assumptions.description: Assumptions about the environment under which this scope is valid. Each assumption should state the consequence if violated. Evidence for SCP-01 score ≥ 3.

drivers_and_principles.title: Drivers and principles
drivers_and_principles.description: Business drivers, requirements, and architecture principles that shape this solution. Evidence for core criterion TRC-01 (major gate). Drivers must be traceable to specific design responses — listing them in isolation scores only 1.

drivers.title: Business drivers
drivers.description: Business motivations this solution addresses. Each driver should link to an architecture_response. Evidence for TRC-01.

architecture_response.title: Architecture response
architecture_response.description: How this solution responds to the driver. Reference the chosen option or a named design mechanism. Linkage is evidence for TRC-01 score ≥ 2.

principles.title: Architecture principles
principles.description: Enterprise or domain principles that constrain the design.

solution_options.title: Solution options and decision
solution_options.description: The optioning work behind the chosen solution. Evidence for profile criterion SOL-01 (major gate — cannot pass above conditional_pass without explicit alternatives and rationale). Score 3 requires a meaningful comparison with explicit selection criteria; score 4 requires criteria-weighted, evidence-backed decisions tied to business and quality concerns.

decision_criteria.title: Decision criteria
decision_criteria.description: The named criteria used to evaluate options. Each criterion should have a weight (or clear priority) so the decision is reproducible. Required for SOL-01 score ≥ 3.

name.title: Criterion name
name.description: Example: 'Delivery speed', 'Strategic fit', 'Total cost of ownership', 'Vendor risk'.

description.title: Description
description.description: What this criterion measures and how it is applied.

options.title: Options considered
options.description: All alternatives evaluated, including the selected one. Documenting only the chosen option is an anti-pattern — score 1 at most. Required for SOL-01 score ≥ 3.

name.title: Option name
name.description: Example: 'Buy — vendor SaaS', 'Build — custom API', 'Extend — middleware layer'.

description.title: Description
description.description: What this option proposes in concrete terms.

criterion_scores.title: Criterion scores
criterion_scores.description: Scores against the named decision criteria.

selected.title: Selected
selected.description: Exactly one option should be marked true.

rejection_reason.title: Rejection reason
rejection_reason.description: Why this option was rejected, with reference to the decision criteria. Required for every non-selected option at SOL-01 score ≥ 3.

chosen_option.title: Chosen option
chosen_option.description: ID or name of the selected option. Must match one of the entries in 'options'.

rationale.title: Selection rationale
rationale.description: Why the chosen option won against the criteria. Reference business drivers, quality attributes, and principles. Required evidence for SOL-01.

tradeoffs_accepted.title: Trade-offs accepted
tradeoffs_accepted.description: Known sacrifices made by choosing this option. Required for SOL-01 score 4.

quality_attributes.title: Quality attributes and NFRs
quality_attributes.description: Measurable targets for each material quality attribute, each with a named architectural mechanism. Evidence for profile criterion SOL-02 (CRITICAL gate — missing or untraceable material NFRs trigger Reject). Score 3 requires most material NFRs to have named architectural mechanisms; score 4 requires every material NFR to be traceable to a specific design decision with measurable targets. At least one quality attribute must be present structurally — an empty array means the SOL-02 evidence slot is unpopulated and cannot be reviewed.

attribute.title: Quality attribute
attribute.description: Concrete, assessable name — not an adjective.

target.title: Measurable target
target.description: Specific number or threshold — not an adjective. Example: '99.9% monthly availability', 'P99 < 200ms under 5K concurrent users', 'EU-only storage'.

quality_scenario.title: Quality scenario
quality_scenario.description: SEI/TOGAF-style scenario: stimulus, source, environment, response, response measure.

architectural_mechanism.title: Architectural mechanism
architectural_mechanism.description: The specific design response that satisfies this NFR. Must be identifiable in the design — named component, pattern, or control. Required evidence for SOL-02. Example: 'Active-passive failover with 30s health check + automated switchover'.

mechanism_location.title: Where in the design
mechanism_location.description: Cross-reference to the section, option, or diagram where the mechanism appears.

validation_strategy.title: Validation strategy
validation_strategy.description: How compliance with this target is validated before and after release. Required for SOL-02 score ≥ 3.

fitness_function.title: Fitness function
fitness_function.description: Automated check that continuously validates this attribute. Required for SOL-02 score 4.

operational_model.title: Operational model and delivery readiness
operational_model.description: How the solution is delivered, operated, and migrated to. Evidence for profile criterion SOL-03. Score 3 requires deployment model, operational ownership, and migration approach to be clear; score 4 requires a complete delivery plan with operating model, observability design, migration steps, and rollback approach.

operating_model.title: Operating model
operating_model.description: Who runs this solution in production and how. Example: 'Jointly operated by Platform Engineering (infra) and Customer Domain squad (application); Platform is on-call for infra incidents, Customer Domain for business logic.'

operational_owner.title: Named operational owner
operational_owner.description: Named team and lead — no unowned solutions. Example: 'Customer Platform Squad — Jane Smith, delivery lead'. Required for SOL-03 score ≥ 3.

strategy.title: Strategy
strategy.description: Example: 'Blue-green via GitHub Actions with manual approval gate on production'.

release_cadence.title: Release cadence
release_cadence.description: Example: 'Weekly release train, emergency hotfix path within 2 hours'.

observability.title: Observability design
observability.description: How the solution is monitored in production. Required for SOL-03 score 4.

migration.title: Migration / rollout approach
migration.description: How the organisation transitions from the current state to the target solution. Required evidence for SOL-03.

approach.title: Approach
approach.description: Example: 'Strangler fig — legacy Payments API deprecated Q4 2026, dual-run period Q3–Q4'.

rollback_plan.title: Rollback plan
rollback_plan.description: How to revert if the rollout fails. Example: 'Feature flag disables new service within 30 seconds; legacy path remains warm for 30 days post-cutover.' Required for SOL-03 score 4.

cutover_criteria.title: Cutover criteria
cutover_criteria.description: Measurable conditions that must be met before promotion to the next phase.

dependencies.title: Implementation dependencies
dependencies.description: Team, platform, or technical dependencies that must be in place for delivery. Required evidence for SOL-03.

raid.title: RAID log (Risks, Assumptions, Issues, Dependencies)
raid.description: Risks, design assumptions, constraints, and trade-offs. Evidence for core criterion RAT-01. Score 3 requires risks with mitigations and owners, constraints documented, and trade-offs explicit.

risks.title: Risks
risks.description: Specific risks with mitigations and named owners. Generic statements score 1.

tradeoffs.title: Trade-offs
tradeoffs.description: Each entry must state what was sacrificed and what was gained. Required for RAT-01 score ≥ 3.

governance.title: Governance and compliance
governance.description: Alignment with applicable standards, policies, and mandatory controls. Evidence for core criterion CMP-01 (critical gate — compliance by assertion scores 1 at most).

compliance_mapping.title: Compliance mapping
compliance_mapping.description: Explicit mapping from each applicable control to the design element that satisfies it. Required for CMP-01 score ≥ 3.

decisions_and_actions.title: Decisions and next actions
decisions_and_actions.description: The governance outcome and follow-on actions. Evidence for core criterion ACT-01. Every action must have a named owner.

next_actions.title: Next actions
next_actions.description: Required for ACT-01 score ≥ 3. Every action must have an owner.

review_triggers.title: Review triggers
review_triggers.description: Conditions that trigger a mandatory re-review of this solution.

glossary.title: Glossary
glossary.description: Canonical definitions for key terms and component names used in this document. Evidence for core criterion CON-01.
